title: Caesar Cipher Part 1: Decoding
body:
The Caesar cipher is a substitution cipher where every letter in the message is shifted along the alphabet by a fixed number of positions. To decode a message, you reverse this shift to bring each letter back to its original place. 
In this lesson, you'll practise decoding messages that have been shifted by a known amount. Focus on how the alphabet “wraps around” when shifts pass the letter A — this wrap-around behaviour is key to restoring the original message.
lesson: CRYPTO-D1A-9f5a2f70, CRYPTO-D1B-55c1a1de, CRYPTO-D4A-8b7f12c9, CRYPTO-D4B-74ad0c21, CRYPTO-D5A-90c3e4e2, CRYPTO-D5B-1f0a76bd
